claims.claim: C1: frequency-separated HRL can share one training core
claims.evidence: Shared dual PPO loop drives Trading and Transit surrogate adapters.
claims.remaining_gap: Copied Transit native simulator still uses copied RESAC runner.

claims.claim: C2: high-level plan variables can be learned as curves
claims.evidence: Upper PPO action can parameterize Bernstein coefficients.
claims.remaining_gap: Public-data and copied-Transit learned plan-coefficient training remain open.

claims.claim: C3: promotion should trigger replanning after persistent shocks
claims.evidence: Promotion-forced plan replan beats interval-only replan on recovery scenario.
claims.remaining_gap: Not yet embedded in learned PPO/off-policy runner.

claims.claim: C4: leakage can be constrained at loss level
claims.evidence: PPO trajectory constraints with primal-dual multiplier reduce lower-LF drift.
claims.remaining_gap: Constraint trades off return/Sharpe and did not improve Transit surrogate drift.

claims.claim: C5: advanced causal encoders can be swapped by domain
claims.evidence: Adaptive lifting wavelet runs in Trading and Transit trackers and ablation.
claims.remaining_gap: Neural state-space and PINN-constrained encoders remain open.

claims.claim: C6: public-data validation covers more than daily bars
claims.remaining_gap: Short Level-1 intraday slice only; no order book or execution simulator.

claims.claim: C7: integrated native Transit Freq-HRL closes the copied-runner gap
claims.evidence: Gap-closure matrix combines count demand state, plan curves, promotion replanning, native lower context, wait credit, and drift constraint.
claims.remaining_gap: Supported on the small Transit surrogate gate; still needs larger native Transit and real-demand validation.

claims.claim: C8: passenger waiting-time frequency credit improves control quality
claims.evidence: Full Freq-HRL path is paired against the same path without wait attribution.
claims.remaining_gap: Supported on the small surrogate gate; still needs larger seed coverage and native timetable validation.

claims.claim: C9: leakage constraints achieve no-tradeoff responsibility separation
claims.evidence: Trading and Transit primal-dual constraints are checked on paired seeds for drift and task-return deltas.
claims.metric: trading drift=supported, trading return=not_supported, transit drift=not_supported, transit reward=not_supported
claims.remaining_gap: The constraint path can reduce drift in trading, but no-tradeoff is not supported and Transit drift is not improved.

claims.claim: C10: dynamic harmonic count-state demand estimator is competitive
claims.evidence: Poisson/NB harmonic estimator is paired against Fourier by seed and source.
claims.remaining_gap: The count-state path is present; it must beat or match Fourier on larger real Transit demand data before becoming a headline claim.

statistical_checks.claim: integrated Transit Freq-HRL improves task reward
integrated Transit Freq-HRL lowers passenger wait proxy
integrated Transit Freq-HRL reduces lower low-frequency drift
frequency-attributed wait credit improves wait proxy
dynamic harmonic count estimator is competitive with Fourier
dynamic harmonic count estimator is competitive with Fourier
dynamic harmonic count estimator is competitive with Fourier
primal-dual constraint lowers trading lower-LF drift
trading leakage constraint has no return tradeoff
primal-dual constraint lowers Transit lower-LF drift
Transit leakage constraint has no reward tradeoff
